title: How do you decrease indent levels in bulleted lists?
excerpt: When working with bulleted lists, you can easily increase the bullet level with TAB, but there is no way to decrease the indent level. Intuitively, this would be done with SHIFT/DEL, but nothing I’ve tried works. So how …

posts.author: mshubes (Michele Shubert)
posts.cookedHtml:
When working with bulleted lists, you can easily increase the bullet level with TAB, but there is no way to decrease the indent level. Intuitively, this would be done with SHIFT/DEL, but nothing I’ve tried works. So how do you accomplish this?

Help?

P.S. I did see another post concerning this issue and it was suggested that the layout feature be used. The layout feature is NOT a valid solution!

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Michele! You should be able to remove the indentation and move the bullet back towards the left of the page with the Return key on your keyboard.

Let me know how this goes!

posts.author: mshubes (Michele Shubert)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t want to REMOVE the indentation, I want to DECREASE it.

posts.author: Hera (ELG)
posts.cookedHtml: Great question that I would like to know the answer to as well. Have you found it yet?

posts.author: Dly215 (Duong )
posts.cookedHtml: Shift+Tab is what I use if I understood correctly. At least this works for me on Mac.

posts.author: mshubes (Michele Shubert)
posts.cookedHtml: I thought I tried every combination possible! This is EXACTLY what I was looking for! Thank you!
